{"query":"Super Bowl LX ticket scams fake betting sites counterfeit lawmakers consumer warning 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1884,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cbsnews.com/news/super-bowl-2026-scams-betting-tickets-merchanise/","title":"Criminals plot to sack Super Bowl fans for a loss. Beware these scams targeting the big game. - CBS News","content":"Super Bowl Sunday this year is sure to feature bone-jarring hits, goofy TV commercials and fraudsters intent on separating you from your money.
Democrats on the congressional panel are warning Americans to beware a range of Super Bowl scams circulating this year.
Americans are expected to wager a record $1.8 billion on Super Bowl LX, according to the American Gaming Association, driven by the explosion in online sports betting.
Bettors should also ensure a site is licensed and regulated by state-based gaming commissions.
Super Bowl tickets this year are a hot commodity as usual, with the cheapest tickets going for about $5,000.
Not surprisingly, scammers are eager to use the strong demand for seats to target consumers, according to lawmakers.
Purchasers could counterfeit digital tickets that will get flagged at the game — or no ticket at all.
Before Super Bowl 54 in 2020, for example, authorities seized more than $120 million worth of counterfeit jerseys, jewelry, hats and other goods as part of a federal sting dubbed "operation team player." Authorities also seized nearly $40 million in counterfeit sports merchandise ahead of last year's Super Bowl."},{"url":"https://www.wral.com/consumer/5onyourside/5-on-your-side-super-bowl-scams-avoid-february-2026/","title":"5 On Your Side: The 4 most common Super Bowl scams to avoid this weekend :: WRAL.com","content":"Fake ticket alerts: A text appearing to be from a legitimate ticket hub like Ticketmaster claiming your tickets to the game could not be delivered.
Streaming account warnings: A text appearing to be from a major streaming platform like Peacock saying something like, "Your Super Bowl stream is on hold.
Freezes to betting accounts: With sports betting still relatively new in North Carolina and millions of us placing bets on the big sites, scammers will send texts like, "Your wager is pending."},{"url":"https://oag.maryland.gov/News/pages/Consumer-Alert---Avoid-Losing-Out-to-a-Super-Bowl-Scam.aspx","title":"Consumer Alert - Avoid Losing Out to a Super Bowl Scam - News - Office of the Attorney General of Maryland","content":"BALTIMORE, MD - Super Bowl Sunday is one of the biggest days of the year for sports.
But with all the excitement comes increased risk of scams and illegal gambling sites looking to take advantage of fans.
Brown wants Maryland consumers to know how to avoid fraud if they plan on wagering on the big game.
Fake Prize Alerts — Watch out for emails or texts claiming you’ve won free tickets or prizes.
Buy merchandise from official retailers.
- They actually pay out when you win — illegal sites may not.
For a full list of Maryland’s legal sportsbook operators and more information about legal wagering, visit the Maryland Lottery and Gaming website.
If you think you’ve been scammed, contact the Consumer Protection Division at 410-528-8662 or visit oag.maryland.gov."},{"url":"https://www.wbay.com/2026/02/06/consumer-first-alert-super-bowl-scams/","title":"Consumer First Alert: Super Bowl scams","content":"Officials say scammers love to use this time to sell fake tickets and merchandise.
When it comes to merchandise, look for the official NFL hologram sticker on the item."}]}
